title: Shoe whitener paste
subtitle: A bicarbonate of soda and hydrogen peroxide paste for cleaning white canvas and rubber shoe surfaces
excerpt: A paste of bicarbonate of soda, 3% hydrogen peroxide, and washing-up liquid removes scuff marks and yellowing from white canvas and rubber soles. Apply with an old toothbrush, leave 3 minutes, and wipe off. Make fresh each use: hydrogen peroxide degrades quickly once mixed with other ingredients and loses its whitening action.
sourceNotes: Shoe whitening paste: bicarbonate of soda as mild abrasive and alkali; hydrogen peroxide 3% (standard pharmacy strength) as oxidising bleach for yellowing and surface stains; washing-up liquid as surfactant to lift dirt. Mix fresh each use; hydrogen peroxide degrades within hours of mixing with bicarbonate. Suitable for canvas, rubber, and synthetic leather; test on small area first for coloured materials.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Mix immediately before use. Do not store mixed paste.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use immediately after mixing. Hydrogen peroxide begins to degrade on contact with bicarbonate of soda. Discard any unused paste.

glossaryTerms.definition: A mild oxidising agent available from pharmacies at 3% concentration (food-safe, sometimes labelled as antiseptic). At this concentration it is safe for household use. It breaks the chemical bonds in yellow and grey stains without stripping fabric colour on white materials.

A cleaning agent that works by releasing oxygen atoms that chemically break down stain molecules. This is distinct from surfactant cleaning (which lifts grease) or abrasive cleaning (which physically scrubs). Hydrogen peroxide is a classic oxidising cleaner.

content.text: that breaks down the yellowing compounds on white rubber and canvas. Bicarbonate of soda adds mild abrasion and boosts the pH slightly. Washing-up liquid lifts the broken-down soil away. Mix only what you need at the moment of use; the combination degrades within minutes and must not be stored.

items.prepNote: 3% concentration from pharmacy; not higher strength
About 1 teaspoon; lifts soil after the peroxide acts

content.text: Stir bicarbonate of soda, hydrogen peroxide, and washing-up liquid together in a small bowl. The mixture will foam slightly. Use immediately; work quickly. Apply to the shoe surface with an old toothbrush using small circular strokes. Work the paste into scuffs and yellow areas.

Leave the paste on the surface for 3 minutes. Wipe off with a damp cloth. For stubborn staining, apply a second application immediately. For canvas uppers, allow the shoe to dry fully before assessing the result; some stains appear to return while still damp.

Test on a hidden area first for any shoe that is not plain white. Not suitable for leather or suede. Hydrogen peroxide at 3% is not strong enough to bleach colour from most synthetic dyes, but a test patch before full application avoids surprises.
